items.title: Axed England star Cole Palmer appears with World Cup superstars in £30m Nike ad blunder featuring Ronaldo and Haaland
items.summary: AXED England star Cole Palmer appears with World Cup aces in a £30million Nike commercial — in an awkward gaffe for the sportswear giant.  The Chelsea midfielder, 24, was picked to line-up with giants of the game including Kylian Mbappe, Cristiano Ronaldo, Vinicius Junior, Virgil van Dijk and E

items.title: 'Absolute madness' - Callaghan fumes at call to not give Turkey red card
items.summary: Former Northern Ireland captain Marissa Callaghan says that Turkey striker Busem Seker "had to be sent off" after she controversially handled the ball before later scoring the winner during their 2-1 victory over Michael McArdle's side.

items.title: Republic of Ireland have nothing to lose against France - Barrett
items.summary: Republic of Ireland striker Amber Barrett says the side "are going to give everything we have" during Tuesday's match against France when they have the chance to secure automatic qualification for the World Cup.

items.title: How England can still qualify for the Women's World Cup
items.summary: England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are all vying for places at the Women's World Cup - but how can they get there with one round of group games left in European qualifying?

items.title: Football Daily
items.summary: Katie Smith is joined by Fran Kirby & Vicki Sparks reacting to England's defeat in Spain.

items.title: Riquelme: Klopp will be first choice for Real Madrid job if I win election
items.summary: Real Madrid presidential candidate Enrique Riquelme has released a statement confirming that Jurgen Klopp will be his choice for the manager's job at the Bernabeu if he is elected on Sunday.

items.title: Advantage Scotland as they await Cuthbert injury news
items.summary: Scotland stay top of their World Cup qualifying group on goal difference from Belgium amid fears of a serious injury to key midfielder Erin Cuthbert.

items.title: Will transfers be a distraction for England at World Cup?
items.summary: England's World Cup campaign is also in the middle of a busy summer transfer window, but how much of a distraction is it?

items.title: Germany forward Karl ruled out of World Cup
items.summary: Germany forward Lennart Karl is ruled out of the 2026 World Cup with a thigh injury.

items.title: Fifa clarifies water bottle stadium rule
items.summary: Fifa says fans will be able to bring sealed disposable water bottles into World Cup stadiums following criticism over a decision to ban reusable bottles.

items.title: Watch: Weir hat-trick as Scotland thump Israel
items.summary: Watch as Scotland thump Israel 6-0 in World Cup qualifying Group B4, with captain Caroline Weir scoring a hat-trick.
